events.title: Hormuz Strait phased reopening
events.description: Vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz are gradually resuming following a phased reopening coordinated by the IMO and Oman, with international efforts to clear stranded ships in a controlled manner.

events.title: KCCI rises 11.88% due to stronger container freight rates
events.description: The Korea Ocean Business Corporation's Container Composite Index (KCCI) increased 11.88% week-on-week to 3,747 points, driven by higher container export spot rates from Busan on major international trade routes, including significant rises on U.S. West Coast and other key routes.

events.title: Ships return to Hormuz Strait central corridor
events.description: The first ships have returned to the central corridor of the Strait of Hormuz, signaling a slow return to normalcy after the war began. However, tanker owners and operators remain cautious.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz conflict disrupts oil supply
events.description: Conflict in the Strait of Hormuz has disrupted oil supply, causing sharp increases in shipping fuel prices and pushing the industry toward alternative fuels.

events.title: Mass evacuation of seafarers trapped in Persian Gulf
events.description: The International Maritime Organization is launching a large-scale evacuation of over 11,000 seafarers stranded in the Persian Gulf following a recent U.S.-Iran peace agreement. The situation highlights a significant disruption to maritime operations in the region.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Phased Evacuation Launched
events.description: The IMO and Oman have initiated a 'phased evacuation' for transits through the Strait of Hormuz, following diplomatic progress between the U.S. and Iran. The move aims to manage traffic and ensure safe passage amid ongoing tensions in the region.

events.title: Dismantling of beached MSC Baltic III in Canada
events.description: Dismantling operations are underway for the beached containership MSC Baltic III in Canada, according to the Canadian Coast Guard. The incident poses a significant vessel-related disruption to local maritime operations.

events.title: MSC updates FAK rates for South Asia to Europe
events.description: MSC has announced new Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates for cargo moving from South Asia to Europe, effective from July 1, 2026. The updated pricing applies to shipments from Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, India, and Pakistan to European gateways including Antwerp and Valencia.

events.title: Hormuz Strait closure risk remains despite ceasefire
events.description: A US-Iran memorandum promises the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, but actual reopening has not occurred, leaving the critical chokepoint at risk of continued closure and disrupting global freight flows.

events.title: US revokes 20,000 Mexican trucker visas
events.description: The U.S. has revoked 20,000 work visas for Mexican truck drivers since April 2025 as part of an expanded crackdown on cabotage, potentially disrupting cross-border freight operations.

events.title: Iran asserts control over Persian Gulf gateway
events.description: Iran is likely to assert control over the gateway to the Persian Gulf, raising concerns about disruptions to crude oil shipments and regional freight flows.

events.title: Iran and Oman Discuss Hormuz Strait Governance
events.description: Iran and Oman have begun formal talks on the future administration of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, including maritime services and associated costs, raising concerns about potential disruptions to this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Suez Canal traffic up amid Hormuz rerouting
events.description: Suez Canal traffic is increasing as cargo reroutes away from the Hormuz Strait due to geopolitical risks, while Egypt's economic growth forecast declines, highlighting a disconnect between shipping activity and fiscal stability.

events.title: Gulf carriers boost airfreight recovery amid high rates
events.description: Gulf carriers are helping to restore airfreight capacity following disruptions from the US-Iran conflict, but global air cargo rates remain elevated despite easing tensions and falling fuel prices.

events.title: Shipping resumes cautiously through Hormuz Strait
events.description: A trickle of container ships is cautiously resuming transit through the Hormuz Strait following a 60-day MoU between the US and Iran. Vessels are navigating mined waters amid ongoing peace talks in Switzerland.

events.title: Increased Tanker Traffic Through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: More tankers are broadcasting their crossings through the Strait of Hormuz, indicating a rise in confidence among shipowners and traders as tensions in the region appear to ease.

events.title: KGTL Port Invests $100M Amid Iran War Cargo Surge
events.description: Pakistan's Karachi Gateway Terminal is planning up to $100 million in new investment over five years due to a cargo surge linked to the Iran war, aiming to reduce freight costs and boost regional shipping.

events.title: China tightens rare earths export rules
events.description: China has placed 10 U.S. companies under stricter rare earths export restrictions, potentially disrupting trade flows and increasing pressure to develop non-Chinese supply chains.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic resuming amid conflicting guidance
events.description: Commercial shipping is resuming through the Strait of Hormuz, but U.S. and Iranian forces are providing conflicting guidance on the safest route, creating uncertainty for freight flows.

events.title: EU ends de minimis customs exemption for low-value imports
events.description: Starting 1 July, the EU will end its de minimis exemption for low-value imports, requiring customs duties on parcels valued under €150. Retailers are warned of potential margin pressures and the need to adjust customs processes.

events.title: Port congestion and rate hikes in July
events.description: Shanghai Port is experiencing significant congestion with delays of four to five days, driven by strong demand for solar panels and the early peak season. This has led to 3.4m teu of box ship capacity being stranded, prompting carriers to plan major rate hikes for July.

events.title: CMA CGM implements new PSS on Far East-East Africa/Seychelles routes
events.description: CMA CGM has announced new Peak Season Surcharges (PSS) effective 1 July 2026 for shipments from the Far East and China to East Africa and the Seychelles, impacting freight costs on these trade routes.

events.title: K Shipbuilding sale collapse
events.description: The sale of South Korean shipbuilder K Shipbuilding has collapsed after negotiations with the sole bidder failed to reach an agreement on deal terms, potentially impacting shipbuilding capacity and supply chain operations.

events.title: US-Iran Hormuz Communication Channel Established
events.description: The US and Iran have set up a direct communication line to prevent incidents and reduce miscommunication in the Strait of Hormuz, following high-level talks in Switzerland.

events.title: US grants Iran oil sanctions waiver
events.description: The Trump administration issued a temporary sanctions waiver allowing the production, sale, and shipment of Iranian oil, marking a significant shift from the 'maximum pressure' policy.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Mine Risk and Dual Transit Regime
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz is open again, but shipping faces challenges due to the presence of mines and a new dual transit regime, complicating the return to normal commercial traffic.

events.title: US-Iran talks ease Hormuz Strait supply fears
events.description: Oil prices fell as progress in US-Iran negotiations eased concerns over potential supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil transit route.

events.title: Explosion at Qatar's key natural gas export terminal
events.description: A major explosion occurred at Qatar's main natural gas export terminal, injuring dozens and leaving 18 people unaccounted for. The incident happened as workers attempted to resume operations following prior damage from an Iranian attack.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz risk framework outdated
events.description: The risk landscape for the Strait of Hormuz has evolved since the 2026 crisis, but compliance frameworks have not kept pace. Vessels still transit the strait, but under changed and heightened risk conditions.

events.title: Russian drones attack ships off Ukraine, killing seafarer
events.description: Russian drones attacked three commercial ships off the coast of Ukraine on June 22, killing an Egyptian seafarer. Ukraine has condemned the attacks, highlighting ongoing disruptions to shipping in the Black Sea.

events.title: U.S. suspends Iranian oil and tanker restrictions
events.description: The U.S. Treasury has temporarily lifted restrictions on Iranian oil and tankers for 60 days, signaling progress in U.S.-Iran negotiations. This policy shift could impact global oil supply chains and tanker operations.

events.title: Gas Plant Blast Injures Dozens in Qatar
events.description: A major blast at the Ras Laffan gas complex in Qatar has killed 13 workers and injured 66 others, raising concerns about industrial safety and potential impacts on energy logistics.

events.title: UPS considers outsourcing UK parcel delivery
events.description: UPS is exploring outsourcing last-mile parcel delivery in the UK to third-party couriers, which could significantly alter labor dynamics and operations for traditional parcel carriers.

events.title: Ocean carriers impose emergency fuel surcharges
events.description: Ocean carriers have introduced emergency bunker surcharges amid the Iran conflict, raising concerns that shippers may be paying double for fuel price increases. This industry move impacts global freight costs and payment structures.

events.title: Port of Virginia deepens harbor to boost container capacity
events.description: The Port of Virginia has completed a $450 million project to deepen its harbor, enhancing its competitiveness as an East Coast container gateway.

events.title: Explosion at Qatar LNG Site During Restart
events.description: Thirteen people were killed and dozens injured in an explosion at Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG complex during a restart of operations following a March Iranian attack. The incident could disrupt LNG production and export activities.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic resumes after weekend closure
events.description: Oil and LNG tankers resumed transits through the Strait of Hormuz after Iran closed the waterway over the weekend, indicating a partial resumption of traffic following the disruption.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz closure disrupts crude oil supply
events.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has disrupted crude oil and heavy residual material supplies to US Gulf Coast refineries, prompting increased reliance on Venezuelan crude to meet processing needs.

events.title: Iran war causes oil demand destruction and potential overhang
events.description: The Iran war has caused a significant drop in global oil demand, according to the IEA. A resolution could lead to a surge in supply and a major oil overhang in 2024.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz oil flow recovery delayed
events.description: Despite a US-Iran deal, oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz face a prolonged recovery due to infrastructure damage and a backlog of tankers. Analysts warn that full normalization of Middle East oil exports will take time.

events.title: Intra-Asia container rates hit 2023 high
events.description: Drewry’s Intra-Asia Container Index rose 1% to $1,114 per 40ft container, reaching its highest level since May 2023. Rates are more than double pre-conflict levels due to regional chokepoint closures and route diversions.

events.title: MOL expects Hormuz Strait transit delays
events.description: Mitsui O.S.K. Lines (MOL) CEO Jotaro Tamura estimates it could take several weeks to a month for normal navigation through the Strait of Hormuz to resume, citing ongoing disruptions.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic discrepancy
events.description: The official traffic count for the Strait of Hormuz no longer aligns with market-tracked data, highlighting a growing divergence that impacts global freight flows.

events.title: Noida Airport opens as alternative to Delhi air cargo congestion
events.description: Noida International Airport (NIA) has begun handling cargo flights, offering an alternative to Delhi Airport amid growing congestion. The airport, operated under a 20-year concession by AISATS, aims to ease capacity constraints in the region.

events.title: Air cargo capacity constraints hinder relief for early peak season
events.description: Tight ocean freight capacity and an early retail peak season are pushing shippers toward air cargo, but limited air freight availability is restricting their ability to shift cargo effectively.

events.title: Yang Ming reports full transpacific and Asia-North Europe freight slots
events.description: Yang Ming announced that freight slots on its transpacific and Asia-North Europe routes are fully booked into July, with demand expected to continue into Q3 due to retailer restocking ahead of Christmas and the expiration of US import tariffs.

events.title: Chile's San Antonio Outer Port Expansion Approved
events.description: Chile has approved a $4.45 billion expansion of the San Antonio Outer Port (El Puerto Exterior), marking the country's largest port development. The project aims to modernize and expand port capacity, potentially impacting regional freight flows and logistics infrastructure.

events.title: Hormuz Strait negotiations and renewed tensions
events.description: Negotiations between the US and Iran to stabilize the Strait of Hormuz continue, following a brief reopening after a pre-deal MoU. The strait closed again due to renewed hostilities in Lebanon, disrupting shipping flows.

events.title: EU ends de minimis customs exemption for low-value imports
events.description: Starting 1 July, the EU will end the de minimis exemption for low-value imports, requiring customs duties of €3 on parcels valued under €150. This policy shift is expected to significantly impact ecommerce logistics and create new operational challenges and opportunities for forwarders.

events.title: Mediterranean spot rate premium surges past $1,500
events.description: The price differential between Asia-North Europe and Asia-Mediterranean spot rates has reached a level not seen since 2022, with the Mediterranean premium exceeding $1,500 per 40ft container, according to the 4 June World Container Index.

events.title: China prepares second LNG terminal for Russian Arctic cargoes
events.description: China is setting up a second import terminal to handle liquefied natural gas from Russia's sanctioned Arctic LNG 2 project, expanding its capacity to process these cargoes beyond a single facility.

events.title: Iranian Crude Exports Surge Through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran has increased crude oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz to the highest level since the war began, as shipping activity rises amid ongoing peace talks between Tehran and Washington.

events.title: Ukrainian attack disrupts Kerch Strait ferry
events.description: Ukraine has attacked the Kerch Strait ferry crossing, causing its shutdown and disrupting Russian fuel supplies. Military truck traffic has been diverted as a result.

events.title: Uncertainty over Hormuz Agreement Stability
events.description: Industry readers express uncertainty about the long-term stability of the Hormuz agreement, indicating ongoing concerns about potential disruptions in this critical shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Nirint removes Halifax from Cuba service route
events.description: Nirint Shipping has removed Halifax from the northbound leg of its Europe–Cuba–Canada multipurpose service, simplifying the route. The service will now skip Halifax in its rotation, impacting freight flows to Canada.

events.title: ANL implements rate restoration on Asia–Australia trade
events.description: ANL has announced a rate restoration programme for shipments from Asia to Australia, effective July 15, 2026. The increase will apply to all dry and refrigerated cargo, added on top of existing Spot and FAK rates.

events.title: Turkish-owned bulker struck by drone in Black Sea
events.description: A Turkish-owned dry bulk vessel was struck by a drone in the Black Sea, causing it to catch fire. The incident underscores heightened risks for commercial shipping amid escalating tensions between Russia and Ukraine.

events.title: Conflicting US-Iran Hormuz Strait Status Reports
events.description: The United States and Iran issued conflicting statements on the status of the Strait of Hormuz, a key energy chokepoint, causing uncertainty and reducing vessel transits below normal levels.

events.title: Iran Shuts Strait of Hormuz, Slows Shipping
events.description: The number of ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz dropped sharply after Iran announced it had again closed the waterway, disrupting a key global shipping route.

events.title: DP World installs large quay cranes at Southampton port
events.description: DP World has received two of Europe's largest quay cranes at Southampton port as part of a £60 million investment to expand capacity. The cranes, each weighing over 2,000 tonnes, arrived fully assembled by sea and will be installed to support operations at the UK's busiest container port.

events.title: Oil Flow Continues Through Strait of Hormuz Despite Iran Closure Claim
events.description: Despite Iran claiming to have shut down the Strait of Hormuz, oil continued to flow through the critical waterway this weekend, highlighting ongoing tensions but no immediate disruption to global oil shipments.

events.title: Tanker Incident Near Al-shihr, Yemen
events.description: A tanker reported being approached by an armed skiff approximately 50 nautical miles southeast of Al-shihr, Yemen, raising concerns about regional security and potential disruptions to maritime trade.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Transit Security Volatility
events.description: Security in the Strait of Hormuz remains highly unstable, with US efforts enabling only a gradual increase in vessel transit. The situation is described as fluctuating 'hour to hour,' indicating ongoing risk for shipping.

events.title: Russian-linked vessels loitering over European subsea cables
events.description: Russian-flagged and linked vessels have been observed zig-zagging, loitering, and taking uneconomic routes above European subsea cables in the Atlantic, with no commercial justification. This behavior raises concerns about potential interference with critical undersea infrastructure.

events.title: Ardmore expands clean tanker fleet amid Hormuz volatility
events.description: Ardmore Shipping increases its newbuild orderbook to four product and chemical tankers, signaling anticipation of ongoing volatility in tanker markets linked to potential disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Reopens After MoU Signing
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz reopened for commercial traffic on June 18 following a U.S.-Iran MoU signed on June 17. A total of 18 vessels transited during the initial window, marking a significant development in regional freight flows.

events.title: Hormuz Strait tensions ease amid fragile US-Iran peace deal
events.description: The Hormuz Strait is seeing a tentative return to normalcy following a peace agreement between the US and Iran, though the situation remains fragile. Shipbroker Gibson noted that after over three months of disruption, the conflict appears closer to resolution.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Closure Disrupts Traffic
events.description: Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to Israeli attacks in Lebanon, causing a significant drop in marine traffic as shipowners comply with the order.

events.title: CMA CGM implements PSS on key trade lanes
events.description: CMA CGM has announced multiple Peak Season Surcharges (PSS) on key trade lanes, including shipments from Asia to Canada, West Africa, the Mediterranean, and North Africa, effective between 22 June and 10 July 2026.

events.title: Maersk extends WAF7 service to South Africa
events.description: Maersk has extended its WAF7 service to include South Africa, adding calls at Port Elizabeth to support the country's fruit export season. The route now connects Mediterranean and West African ports with South Africa.

events.title: New direct rail link launched between Baltic Hub and Ostrava
events.description: Loconi Intermodal has launched a direct weekly rail service from the Baltic Hub in Gdańsk to the PKP Cargo International Terminal in Ostrava, Czech Republic, starting in May 2026. This marks the first direct rail connection between the two terminals.

events.title: MSC launches Sierra service for Far East-Mexico route
events.description: MSC has launched the Sierra service, a new Far East–Mexico route complementing its Mexicas loop. The service operates between Shanghai, Qingdao, Busan, Manzanillo, and Lazaro Cardenas.

events.title: Maersk adjusts Far East-Bangladesh shipping routes
events.description: Maersk has revised its Far East–Bangladesh service network, including changes to the SH1, SH2, SH3, and IA7 services. The adjustments aim to optimize coverage and include the removal of calls at Hong Kong for the revised SH1 service.

events.title: Iran resumes Kharg Island oil loadings
events.description: Iran has resumed crude oil loading at its Kharg Island terminal after a six-week halt caused by a US-imposed blockade. The resumption follows the lifting of the US restrictions, impacting global oil supply chain operations.

events.title: CNC Line revises KCM2 service
events.description: CNC Line has revised its Korea–China–Malaysia (KCM2) service, removing calls at Shantou and the second Busan call, while adding Xiamen. The fleet has been reduced from seven to six vessels.

events.title: Maersk replaces ONE as Adriatic service vessel provider
events.description: Maersk will replace Ocean Network Express (ONE) as the vessel provider on the jointly operated Adriatic Service (AD1), which it will now market as the Adriatic Sea. ONE will remain as a slot charterer with limited port coverage.

events.title: US Monitors Hormuz Strait to Ensure Open Passage
events.description: The U.S. military is monitoring the Strait of Hormuz to ensure it remains open, countering Iran's claims that it had closed the critical shipping passage.

events.title: Americold opens cold chain hub at Port Saint John
events.description: Americold has opened a new integrated cold chain facility at Port Saint John in collaboration with DP World and Canadian Pacific Kansas City. The hub combines cold storage, port operations, and rail services to improve the efficiency of perishable freight movement.

events.title: Drone attack on Panama-flagged ship in Black Sea
events.description: A drone attack on a Panama-flagged ship in the Black Sea killed one crew member and injured two others, according to Panama's Maritime Authority.

events.title: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz to Shipping
events.description: Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz for shipping due to what it described as a violation of a ceasefire. The move comes amid ongoing tensions and could severely disrupt global oil and freight flows.

events.title: Ships Allowed to Use South Hormuz Route with Signals On
events.description: Ships are now permitted to transit the southern route of the Strait of Hormuz at any time of day provided their signals are on, indicating a potential shift in navigation protocols in the critical chokepoint.

events.title: Allianz Wasserstraße launched to boost inland waterways
events.description: Rhenus Logistics, duisport, HGK Shipping, and Viking Cruises have formed the Allianz Wasserstraße alliance to strengthen inland waterways as a sustainable transport network for European supply chains.

events.title: Shadowy Vessel Intercepted Near Solomon Islands
events.description: A joint operation by the Royal Solomon Islands Police Force, Customs, and Immigration intercepted a suspicious vessel suspected of international crimes, potentially impacting regional maritime security and trade.

events.title: Panama Canal conducts Gatun Locks maintenance
events.description: The Panama Canal is performing scheduled maintenance on a Gatun Locks chamber using a dry chamber operation. The maintenance is part of a long-term infrastructure program and is being carried out without disrupting transits.

events.title: MSC restructures intra-Far East shipping network
events.description: MSC is restructuring its intra-Far East network by discontinuing the Bayan Ko service and revising regional loops to maintain coverage between the Pearl River Delta, Vietnam, and the Philippines.

events.title: Bulker Contacts Wharf During Departure
events.description: The bulk carrier Pacific Century contacted a wharf during departure due to miscommunication between the master and pilot, causing a disruption in port operations.

events.title: Increased pirate aggression with gunfire in Somali waters
events.location: Gulf of Aden and Somali Coast
events.description: Pirates are using increased aggression, including gunfire, to board ships in the Gulf of Aden and along the Somali coast. Authorities have warned of the rising threat to maritime safety in the region.

events.title: Norfolk Harbor Dredging Project Completed
events.description: The $450 million Norfolk Harbor dredging project has been completed, improving vessel access and capacity at Norfolk International Terminals.

events.title: Iran establishes second unnamed chokepoint
events.description: Iran's Quds Force has constructed and tested a second chokepoint in the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about potential disruptions to global shipping despite the strait reopening toll-free.

events.title: Baltimore Bridge Collision with Dali
events.description: The chief engineer of the containership Dali admitted to criminal conduct related to the vessel's collision with the Francis Scott Key Bridge in Baltimore. The incident caused significant disruption to local supply chain operations and raised legal and regulatory concerns.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic surges amid AIS disruption
events.description: On 18 June, 25 commercial vessels crossed the Strait of Hormuz — the highest daily count in two months, more than five times the average for early June. This increase occurs amid severe AIS disruption, complicating tracking and raising concerns over regional freight flows.

events.title: CLdN acquires Samskip's freight services
events.description: CLdN has completed the acquisition of Samskip’s quay-to-quay and door-to-door freight services between the Continent and the UK & Ireland. The services will integrate into the CLdN network starting 29th June, including container shipping operations from the Dutch port.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening after conflict
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz is reopening following a nearly four-month conflict, easing the acute threat to global energy supplies. However, analysts warn that economic damage is already significant and will take months to resolve.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz container shipping recovery delayed
events.description: The US-Iran deal is expected to reopen container shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, but full recovery is at least three months away, with ocean supply chain networks not expected to normalize before mid-September 2026.

events.title: Shipping groups call for mine clearance in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Major shipping organizations have called for mine clearance and restoration of traditional transit routes in the Strait of Hormuz to restore normal maritime traffic. Iran has warned of mine risks, raising concerns over the safety and coordination of vessel passage.

events.title: Confirmed Mine in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A confirmed mine has been reported in the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns among shipowners despite increased traffic in the region. The presence of the mine introduces significant uncertainty and risk to global shipping flows through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Mexican Patrol Boat Collides with Docked Ferry
events.description: A Mexican patrol boat lost control and collided with a docked ferry at the port of Isla Mujeres on June 18. No injuries were reported.

events.title: Japanese Ro-Ro Ferry Grounds Near Tokyo
events.description: A Japanese roll-on/roll-off cargo ferry ran aground overnight while traveling from Osaka to Tokyo. There were no immediate reports of damage or environmental impact.

events.title: New Iraq-UAE logistics service launched via Khalifa Port
events.description: AD Ports Group has launched a weekly direct shipping service for container and Ro-Ro cargo between Khalifa Port in the UAE and Umm Qasr Port in Iraq, enhancing regional trade connectivity.

events.title: Oil shipments rise in Hormuz amid Iran transit concerns
events.description: Oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz increased following a U.S.-Iran ceasefire deal, though concerns remain over Iran's transit terms for the critical waterway.

events.title: India's LNG carrier Disha docks after Hormuz blockade
events.description: India's first LNG carrier, Disha, arrived at Dahej Port after being blocked for three months in the Strait of Hormuz. The government is coordinating to facilitate the safe return of Indian vessels and energy cargoes from the Persian Gulf.

events.title: U.S. lifts blockade, allows ships to Iranian ports
events.description: The U.S. Navy has allowed more than a dozen ships to pass through to Iranian ports, lifting a blockade as part of an agreement to end the war, according to Vice President JD Vance.

events.title: Russia attacks ships off Ukraine, killing seafarer
events.description: A Russian attack on commercial ships near Ukraine's seaports resulted in one seafarer killed and others injured, according to Ukrainian officials. The escalation raises concerns over the safety of maritime trade in the region.

events.title: Iran Imposes 'Toll by Insurance' in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran is escalating tensions in the Strait of Hormuz by implementing a new 'toll by insurance' policy, increasing the risk of disruptions to global shipping and energy flows through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz shipping normalises
events.description: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz is beginning to normalise, easing a major disruption to global crude oil flows and contributing to a decline in oil prices.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz re-opening expected to boost tanker rates
events.description: Scorpio Tankers anticipates oil tanker rates will rise above pre-conflict levels as markets prepare for the re-opening of the Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: U.S. imports from Hormuz-affected ports drop 93%
events.description: U.S. maritime imports from ports affected by the Strait of Hormuz disruption fell 93.2% year-on-year in May, according to Descartes, highlighting the significant impact of the waterway's closure on trade flows.

events.title: Thailand revives $30bn land bridge as Malacca alternative
events.description: Thailand is reviving a $30 billion land bridge project to serve as an alternative to the Malacca Strait, focusing on feeder cargo rather than mainline traffic. The initiative faces challenges including financing, political opposition, and Chinese influence.

events.title: Versailles Deal Impact on Strait Reopening Uncertain
events.description: The interim Versailles Deal signed by Trump leaves the strait's reopening status unconfirmed, with mine clearance still pending, creating uncertainty for maritime freight flows.

events.title: Bangladesh allows private air cargo operators
events.description: Bangladesh is permitting private cargo operator stations near international airports to address airfreight congestion and improve export efficiency, as announced in the national budget.

events.title: Chinese carriers convert bulk ships to container vessels
events.description: Chinese carriers, including the Cosco group, are converting bulk carriers into container ships to address long newbuilding wait times and capitalize on the strong charter market. Conversions are expected to be completed between Q3 and Q4 2024.

events.title: Gulf land bridge capacity expansion
events.description: DHL, Oman Air Cargo, and GWC are expanding capacity on the Gulf land bridge, including new daily Road Feeder Services and TIR-powered logistics, as global supply chains adjust to disruptions from the US/Israeli conflict with Iran.

events.title: Container freight rates surge in July 2024
events.description: Container carriers have pushed through fresh rate hikes in July, leading to double-digit increases in spot rates on transpacific and Asia-Europe routes. The Drewry World Container Index reported a 15% weekly rise on the Shanghai-Rotterdam route and a 12% increase on the Shanghai-Genoa route.

events.title: Iran oil exports resume as blockade lifts
events.description: A months-long US naval blockade on Iranian ports has been lifted, allowing seven supertankers to sail with crude oil exports resuming. This marks a significant shift in regional freight flows and trade dynamics.

events.title: EU bans EU operators from Russian LNG trade globally
events.description: The EU has closed a loophole in its Russian LNG ban, prohibiting EU shipowners and buyers from transporting, trading, or marketing Russian LNG globally. This policy move significantly impacts European energy buyers and shipping companies.

events.title: WEC Lines launches crossdocking service in Hamburg
events.description: WEC Lines has introduced a new crossdocking solution at the C. Steinweg (Süd-West) Terminal in Hamburg, enhancing cargo connectivity between Germany, the Netherlands, Portugal, Spain, and the Canary Islands.

events.title: Port of NY/NJ cargo volumes decline in April
events.location: Port of New York and New Jersey
events.description: Container volumes at the Port of New York and New Jersey dropped 8.4% year-over-year in April, returning to more typical levels after a record March.

events.title: Grande Torino exits Persian Gulf after 100+ days
events.description: Grimaldi Group's PCTC Grande Torino has safely transited the Strait of Hormuz after being stranded in the Persian Gulf for over 100 days due to regional tensions. The vessel received authorization from Iran to depart.

events.title: BNSF approves $4B inland rail hub in Barstow
events.description: Barstow City Council approved BNSF Railway's $4 billion Barstow International Gateway, a major inland rail hub combining a rail yard, intermodal terminal, and transload warehouses to improve freight flow from the ports.

events.title: Dali chief engineer admits criminal conduct in Key Bridge incident
events.description: The chief engineer of the containership Dali admitted to criminal conduct related to the vessel's collision with Baltimore's Francis Scott Key Bridge, which caused significant disruption to local logistics and raised safety concerns.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz transits resume after four-month shutdown
events.description: Vessel tracking services recorded 17 transits through the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, with two more in the early hours of Friday, marking a tentative resumption of activity in a waterway effectively closed for nearly four months. However, the diplomatic agreement supporting the reopening is showing signs of instability.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz partial reopening amid risks
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz saw increased transits, with 25 recorded on Thursday and more on Friday, marking the highest single-day count in months. However, diplomatic tensions and mine risks continue to cloud the reopening after nearly four months of disruption.

events.title: Russian Frigate Drifts in English Channel
events.description: A Russian frigate, low on fuel, is drifting in the English Channel after failing to intervene in a Royal Navy operation to seize a sanctioned vessel on June 14.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Passage Fee Proposal
events.description: The possibility of new tolls for vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz remains unresolved, with implications for global shipping and U.S.-Iran relations.

events.title: Hormuz Strait negotiations resume, shipping resumes
events.description: The US declared an end to its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, allowing shipping to resume as negotiations with Iran over Tehran’s nuclear program begin. The development follows the implementation of a US-Iran interim peace deal.

events.title: Fuel availability tight in Houston port
events.description: Fuel availability is slightly tight at the Houston port, with lead times for HSFO, LSMGO, and VLSFO extending to 5–7 days, according to a trader.

events.title: Interferry urges pragmatic EES enforcement to avoid border chaos
events.description: Interferry warns of potential congestion at EU borders due to the new Entry/Exit System (EES), urging authorities to adopt a flexible approach to avoid disruptions during the summer peak travel season.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening delays shipping backlog clearance
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz is set to reopen, but industry experts warn it may take weeks to clear the shipping backlog. The situation is also affecting oil prices and global freight flows.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz closure impacts fertilizer shipments
events.description: Global fertilizer shipments have dropped 11% due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, restricting exports from the Persian Gulf. A potential US-Iran ceasefire could ease the disruption and restore flows.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz navigation resumption uncertain
events.description: Despite an imminent deal between the U.S. and Iran, the shipping industry remains cautious about resuming normal navigation through the Strait of Hormuz. Insurance markets are hesitant, and Germany has deployed naval vessels for potential mine clearance.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz safe passage agreement signed
events.description: A possible agreement between the US and Iran to end hostilities and ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz has been signed, but the industry is awaiting detailed implementation plans.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening analysis
events.description: Analysis suggests a potential Strait of Hormuz reopening could impact VLCC tonnage flows, with existing ballast capacity and possible Atlantic route diversions affecting freight dynamics.

events.title: Asia-US container rates rising due to peak season and tariffs
events.description: Container shipping rates from Asia to the US are under upward pressure due to an early peak season and volume shifts ahead of new tariffs. However, falling fuel costs may help limit the rate increases.

events.title: MSC Phoenix service launched at Port of Rijeka
events.description: Adriatic Gate Container Terminal has launched the MSC Phoenix service at the Port of Rijeka, enhancing direct connectivity to the Far East. This development strengthens Rijeka's role as a strategic gateway in the Adriatic region.

events.title: Teamsters files grievances against UPS over nonunion drivers
events.description: The Teamsters union plans to file grievances against UPS for allegedly using nonunion gig drivers via its subsidiary Roadie, which could disrupt freight delivery operations and lead to labor disputes.

events.title: VOC Port Authority sets new berthing safety norms
events.description: The VOC Port Authority has introduced minimum tug and pilot deployment requirements for safe berthing operations, based on vessel type, length, and draft, effective immediately.

events.title: NYK, JERA charter VLGCs for low-carbon ammonia transport from U.S. to Japan
events.description: NYK Bulkship (Asia) has signed a time-charter contract with JERA to transport low-carbon ammonia from the U.S. to Japan using two very large gas carriers. This marks a significant industry move toward alternative fuel transportation for power generation.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening agreement reached
events.description: An agreement has been reached to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint. The World Shipping Council emphasized the importance of ensuring safe passage for stranded vessels and crews.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz MoU Progress
events.description: A Memorandum of Understanding signed by the U.S. and Iran aims to ensure the security and unimpeded flow of commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: U.S. Seizes Tanker After Weeks-Long Pursuit
events.description: A tanker previously seized by the U.S. in a prolonged pursuit has reemerged under mysterious circumstances. The vessel's reappearance raises questions about its current status and potential implications for maritime security and trade flows.

events.title: Rhenus builds new Dortmund transshipment hub
events.description: Rhenus Road Freight has received approval to construct a new transshipment hub in Dortmund, Germany, set to open in 2027. The facility will replace an existing site in Unna and marks the company's first in-house built transshipment center in Germany.

events.title: Hormuz Strait risk profile hardening
events.description: Diplomatic and military descriptions of the situation at the Strait of Hormuz show increasing divergence, with the risk profile inside the framework hardening, raising concerns for global shipping.

events.title: Thailand revives land bridge as Malacca alternative
events.description: Thailand is advancing a $30.5 billion land bridge project to connect the Gulf of Thailand with the Andaman Sea, aiming to provide an alternative to the Strait of Malacca amid regional disruptions.

events.title: Valencia Port Cargo Volume Drops 3.59% in First Five Months
events.description: Valenciaport handled 32.8 million metric tons of cargo in January-May 2025, a 3.59% decline compared to the same period in 2024. The drop was primarily driven by a nearly 60% decrease in solid bulk cargo, particularly construction materials.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Reopening After U.S.-Iran Deal
events.description: A U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding has opened the door to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz after months of conflict. While welcomed, the shipping industry warns that full normalization of operations may take time.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Management Questions Post-Iran Deal
events.description: A new US-Iran memorandum of understanding raises concerns about the future management and stability of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Mine Risks Delay Vessel Reopening
events.description: Maritime organizations are calling for clear guidance from the Trump administration on how vessels can safely exit the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing mine risks. The uncertainty is causing delays and safety concerns for stranded ships.

events.title: U.S. Ends Maritime Blockade of Iran
events.description: The United States has officially ended its maritime blockade of Iran, with enforcement operations targeting vessels entering or leaving Iranian ports now ceased. This policy shift may impact regional freight flows and trade dynamics in the Persian Gulf.

events.title: US-Iran deal eases Strait of Hormuz tensions
events.description: A deal between the US and Iran to halt hostilities and reopen traffic through the Strait of Hormuz offers limited relief to European gas and LNG markets, though logistical hurdles and geopolitical uncertainty remain.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening after U.S. blockade lifted
events.description: An interim deal announced by President Trump will lift the U.S. blockade and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, leading to normalization of Persian Gulf oil exports by end of July, a month earlier than previously expected.

events.title: US-Iran deal reopens Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A fast-tracked US-Iran peace agreement has led to the immediate reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and a halt to fighting, causing oil prices to drop significantly as tensions ease.

events.title: US-Iran interim deal eases Hormuz Strait concerns
events.description: An interim US-Iran peace deal has eased supply concerns by potentially reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for metal exports from the Persian Gulf, leading to a drop in aluminum prices.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening eases coal trade tensions
events.description: A US-Iran interim peace agreement paves the way for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, easing concerns over energy and freight flows. This development has led to a decline in thermal coal prices as risks to this critical maritime chokepoint diminish.

events.title: DP World to Develop Corpus Christi Container Terminal
events.description: DP World has entered exclusive negotiations for a long-term lease to develop and operate a container terminal at the Port of Corpus Christi, Texas. This development aims to expand containerized cargo capabilities and capture new trade flows.

events.title: Corn ship enters Persian Gulf after crossing US blockade line
events.description: A grain vessel carrying corn to Iran has entered the Persian Gulf after crossing the US blockade line outside the Strait of Hormuz, signaling a potential easing of tensions ahead of a planned US-Iran interim peace agreement.

events.title: Hanwha LNG carriers blocked from delivery to Russia
events.description: Six Hanwha Ocean-built LNG icebreakers remain undelivered to Russia due to sanctions on its Arctic energy project, causing significant financial strain on the shipbuilder.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz shipping recovery delayed
events.description: Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is unlikely to return to normal immediately despite a U.S.-Iran agreement to reopen the key waterway, according to Mitsui OSK Lines CEO Jotaro Tamura.

events.title: US-Iran truce may ease Hormuz Strait shipping tensions
events.description: Analysts suggest a potential US-Iran truce could restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, though broader issues like Iran's nuclear program remain unresolved. Iran has previously demonstrated its ability to disrupt shipping in the region.

events.title: DP World to enter U.S. container market via Corpus Christi deal
events.description: Global ports operator DP World is pursuing a deal to enter the U.S. container and ports market through a partnership with Corpus Christi. This move signals a strategic expansion into a key North American logistics hub.

events.title: Potential new U.S. tariff changes in July
events.description: Trade experts at Flexport warn that new tariffs and compliance requirements could replace expiring duties by late July, potentially causing legal and logistical disruptions in U.S. trade.

events.title: U.S.-Iran MOU Aims to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A 14-point U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding could lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, easing global supply chain disruptions caused by recent regional tensions.

events.title: Shanghai port congestion raises intra-Asia freight rates
events.description: Shanghai port congestion is driving up intra-Asia freight rates, with the Shanghai-Southeast Asia rate rising 5% in a single week and nearly 50% year-on-year. The slow return of empty containers and ongoing congestion are contributing to the increase.

events.title: Larger vessels deployed on Intra-Europe routes
events.description: Container lines are increasingly using larger vessels (over 8,000 TEU) on intra-Europe routes, with a 78% year-on-year increase in such ships operating in the Mediterranean. This shift reflects network restructuring by carriers like MSC.

events.title: US-Iran MoU sparks optimism, Hormuz traffic rises
events.description: The US and Iran have signed a deal to end the war, leading to cautious optimism and increased ocean transits through the Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: US firms sue container makers over price-fixing
events.description: Two US companies have filed class-action lawsuits against container manufacturers accused of price-fixing in the global dry container market, following allegations from American prosecutors.

events.title: Saudi tankers pass Hormuz after Iran deal
events.description: Three Saudi-flagged supertankers carrying six million barrels of crude passed through the Strait of Hormuz following a U.S.-Iran war-ending deal, indicating a potential easing of tensions in a key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Qatar returns empty LNG ship via Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Qatar brought an empty liquefied natural gas tanker back into the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz for the first time since the war in Iran began, signaling potential changes in LNG export strategies amid regional tensions.

events.title: Fatal Accident at Zeebrugge Ro/Ro Freighter
events.description: A docker was killed in a cargo loading accident aboard a ro/ro freighter at the Port of Zeebrugge, Belgium. The incident is under investigation.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopens under US-Iran deal
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz has reopened following a US-Iran interim agreement, but uncertainty remains about implementation and potential impacts on shipping transit fees and operations.

events.title: Hormuz Strait shipping remains low after Iran-U.S. war deal
events.description: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remained minimal two days after a U.S.-Iran agreement to end hostilities, with only four vessel crossings recorded by maritime trackers.

events.title: Hormuz insurance costs impact product tankers more
events.description: High war-risk insurance costs are deterring clean oil product tankers more than crude carriers from transiting the Strait of Hormuz, due to relatively smaller increases in product freight rates compared to crude.

events.title: ARA Bunkering Hub Fuel Availability Tight
events.description: Fuel availability for HSFO and VLSFO remains tight at the ARA bunkering hub due to terminal loading delays, with recommended lead times of 7-8 days. LSMGO stems also require around six days of notice.

events.title: UAE Reducing Oil Export Reliance on Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The UAE is working to reduce its dependence on the Strait of Hormuz for crude oil exports, diverting shipments via pipeline systems to mitigate risks associated with this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Interim Iran Deal Reopens Strait of Hormuz
events.description: President Trump signed an interim deal to end the conflict with Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, despite opposition from some Republicans.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic increasing but not normal
events.description: Traffic in the Strait of Hormuz showed signs of increasing on June 16 and 17, though it remains significantly below pre-war levels, indicating ongoing disruption to global shipping flows.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd updates CCM feeder service
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd has introduced a dedicated weekly feeder to improve schedule reliability for its CCM feeder service connecting Mexico and El Salvador. The updated service, effective July 10, 2026, simplifies the rotation between Lazaro Cardenas and Acajutla.

events.title: BNSF approves $4B California rail intermodal project
events.description: A local city council has approved BNSF's $4 billion intermodal rail project, aimed at enhancing freight capacity from the busiest U.S. container complex.

events.title: US-Iran interim deal may impact Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The US and Iran are preparing to sign an interim peace deal, with energy insiders skeptical about how quickly it can stabilize the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Ships Stranded in Persian Gulf Require Barnacle Cleaning
events.description: Ships stranded in the Persian Gulf for over three months are requiring urgent barnacle removal by specialized divers ahead of their departure, causing increased demand for marine cleaning services.

events.title: Gulf supply crisis impacts Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The International Energy Agency (IEA) lowered its global oil demand forecast due to a Gulf supply crisis, citing disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz. It expects supply through the chokepoint to gradually recover to 8 million barrels a day by 2027.

events.title: Ukraine strikes sanctioned crude oil tanker in Black Sea
events.description: Ukrainian forces reportedly struck a sanctioned crude oil tanker in the Black Sea overnight. The incident may disrupt regional shipping and raise concerns over maritime security in the area.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening possible under interim US-Iran deal
events.description: An interim US-Iran peace deal could lead to the Strait of Hormuz being reopened within 30 days, with a 60-day window for a final agreement. This development may ease current shipping disruptions in the region.

events.title: Iranian crude exports resume after Hormuz deal
events.description: Iranian crude oil exports are resuming as Iranian-linked tankers reappear and exit through the Gulf of Oman following a two-month naval blockade and a recent Hormuz deal.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Crisis Disrupts Oil Trade
events.description: Months of conflict near the Strait of Hormuz have significantly disrupted oil trade flows, leading to a sharp downgrade in global oil demand projections by the International Energy Agency.

events.title: U.S.-Iran Hormuz Strait Conflict Set to End
events.description: The U.S. and Iran have agreed to end the conflict in the Strait of Hormuz, with a 60-day toll-free transit window. The U.S. blockade on Iranian ports remains in place until the agreement is formally signed.

events.title: Rising container shipping costs to Middle East
events.description: Container shipping costs from South Korea to the Middle East increased for the third consecutive month in May, with a 4.9% monthly rise to an average of 6.81 million won (US$4,520) for a 40-foot container.

events.title: Suez Canal transits increase after Iran conflict
events.description: The number of containerships transiting the Suez Canal increased to 27 in the week ended 7 June and 28 in the week ended 14 June, according to the Drewry Red Sea Diversion Tracker. The recovery follows a diplomatic agreement after the Iran conflict, potentially signaling a return to normal freight flows.

events.title: Resurgent Somali Piracy in Western Indian Ocean
events.description: Somali piracy has increased sharply in the first half of 2026, with Pirate Action Groups showing renewed confidence after years of suppression, posing a significant risk to commercial shipping in the region.

events.title: Maersk PH6 service launches at Visayas Container Terminal
events.description: The Visayas Container Terminal in the Philippines has welcomed the inaugural call of Maersk's PH6 service, enhancing trade connectivity for the Visayas region and providing direct access to key international trade hubs.

events.title: DP World to build first U.S. Gulf Coast container terminal
events.description: DP World has entered exclusive negotiations to develop and operate a container terminal at the Port of Corpus Christi in Texas. This marks the company's first container terminal on the U.S. Gulf Coast.

events.title: Maersk resumes Red Sea transits amid vessel shortage
events.description: Maersk Line has restarted Red Sea transits after a three-month pause, as vessel availability remains constrained and charter rates rise. The Santa Catarina Maersk and Clementine Maersk are the first of the operator’s ships to pass through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait in this period.

events.title: Early peak shipping surge in Los Angeles
events.description: Importers are frontloading cargo to Los Angeles ahead of rising fuel costs and shifting U.S. trade policies, causing an early peak in the shipping season and increased port activity.

events.title: FedEx operational recovery in Vietnam
events.description: FedEx is gradually resolving cargo backlogs in Vietnam following a problematic transition to a new ground delivery partner and technology system.

events.title: CargoWise global outage disrupts supply chain operations
events.description: CargoWise users worldwide experienced a two-hour outage, causing login failures and disrupting electronic messaging. WiseTech Global declared a 'Major Incident' and implemented remediation.

events.title: Zanzibar plans new seaport and free zone
events.description: Zanzibar is developing an integrated seaport and free zone at Mangapwani to enhance freight movement and boost trade, aiming to reestablish its maritime role.

events.title: Ennore Port second container terminal development
events.description: Ennore Port, near Chennai, is planning a second container terminal, drawing interest from major global carriers. The development aims to boost capacity and efficiency amid growing cargo volumes.

events.title: DP World to build container terminal in Corpus Christi
events.description: DP World has entered exclusive negotiations to develop and operate a container terminal at Corpus Christi, marking its entry into the US market. The port currently lacks container handling capabilities and this development could significantly impact regional freight flows.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening ahead of US-Iran deal
events.description: Tanker and LNG fleets are repositioning toward the Gulf in anticipation of the Strait of Hormuz reopening following a planned US-Iran peace agreement signing in Geneva.

events.title: Strong El Niño expected to disrupt global shipping
events.description: NOAA has declared the formation of a strong El Niño in the tropical Pacific, which could intensify into one of the strongest on record. This weather phenomenon is expected to significantly impact global shipping routes and freight flows.

events.title: Iranian tankers bypass U.S. blockade
events.description: Iranian-owned 'shadow fleet' tankers have begun moving past the U.S. Navy's de facto blockade line in the Gulf of Oman, signaling ongoing tensions affecting regional shipping and oil flows.
